results.rendered_html: yūpa m. (prob. fr. √yup; but according to  Uṇ. iii, 27 , fr. √2. yu) a post, beam, pillar, (esp.) a smooth post or stake to which the sacrificial victim is fastened, any sacrificial post or stake (usually made of bamboos or Khadira wood; in  R. i, 13, 24 ; 25 , where the horse sacrifice is described, 21 of these posts are set up, 6 made of Bilva, 6 of Khadira, 6 of Palāśa, one of Uḍumbara, one of Śleṣmātaka, and one of Deva-dāru),  RV.  &c. &c.
